events.title: Claude Shannon publishes plan for a chess-playing computer
events.summary: Shannon's paper 'Programming a Computer for Playing Chess' lays out minimax search and evaluation functions, framing game-playing as a proving ground for machine intelligence.

events.title: Norbert Wiener popularizes cybernetics for a general audience
events.summary: Wiener's 'The Human Use of Human Beings' brings cybernetics — feedback, control, and communication in machines and organisms — into public debate about automation and society.

events.title: Turing publishes 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence'
events.summary: Alan Turing's Mind paper asks whether machines can think and proposes the imitation game, later known as the Turing test, as an operational answer. It frames the field's central question decades before the field exists.

events.title: Minsky and Edmonds build SNARC, an early neural-net machine
events.summary: Marvin Minsky and Dean Edmonds construct a 40-neuron analog machine that learns to run a maze using reinforcement, one of the first hardware neural network experiments.

events.title: Georgetown-IBM demo shows machine translation of Russian
events.summary: IBM and Georgetown University publicly demonstrate automatic Russian-to-English translation of about 60 sentences, sparking optimism and funding for machine translation.

events.title: Dartmouth workshop founds artificial intelligence as a field
events.summary: John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Claude Shannon, and Nathaniel Rochester convene the summer research project that names 'artificial intelligence' and sets its founding agenda.

events.title: Dartmouth workshop founds the field of AI
events.summary: McCarthy, Minsky, Shannon and Rochester convene the summer workshop that names and launches artificial intelligence as a discipline.

events.title: Newell and Simon demonstrate the Logic Theorist
events.summary: The Logic Theorist proves theorems from Principia Mathematica, becoming one of the first working AI programs and an early demonstration of heuristic search.

events.title: Newell, Shaw, and Simon create the General Problem Solver
events.summary: GPS attempts to solve formalized problems with means-ends analysis, an influential early attempt at a domain-general reasoning program.

events.title: John McCarthy creates the LISP programming language
events.summary: LISP becomes the dominant language of AI research for decades, introducing ideas like symbolic expressions, garbage collection, and code-as-data.

events.title: Rosenblatt demonstrates the perceptron
events.summary: The Mark I perceptron shows a machine can learn from examples, seeding six decades of neural-network research.

events.title: Rosenblatt unveils the perceptron, a machine that learns
events.summary: Frank Rosenblatt demonstrates the perceptron, a trainable neural network for pattern recognition, to the US Navy and press. It establishes learning from data as a path to machine intelligence.

events.title: McCarthy creates LISP
events.summary: LISP becomes the workhorse language of AI research for the next three decades.

events.title: Arthur Samuel's checkers program coins 'machine learning'
events.summary: Samuel publishes his study of a self-improving checkers player at IBM and introduces the term 'machine learning', an early demonstration that programs can outplay their authors.

events.title: McCarthy and Minsky start the MIT AI Project
events.summary: The lab that becomes the MIT AI Laboratory is founded, anchoring one of the field's most influential research communities.

events.title: Samuel's checkers program learns from play
events.summary: Arthur Samuel publishes machine-learning checkers that improves beyond its author, coining 'machine learning'.

events.title: DARPA funds Project MAC at MIT
events.summary: ARPA grants MIT roughly $2 million for Project MAC, beginning two decades of largely unrestricted US defense funding that sustains early AI research.

events.title: Gordon Moore predicts exponential growth in transistor counts
events.summary: Moore's Electronics article observes that transistor density doubles roughly every year (later revised to two), the compute trend that ultimately underwrites deep learning.

events.title: Stanford launches DENDRAL, the first expert system project
events.summary: Feigenbaum, Lederberg, and colleagues begin DENDRAL to infer molecular structures from mass spectrometry, pioneering knowledge-based systems.

events.title: I. J. Good describes an 'intelligence explosion'
events.summary: Statistician I. J. Good argues that an ultraintelligent machine could design still better machines, an early formal statement of ideas that later shape AI safety debates.

events.title: Weizenbaum's ELIZA chatbot mimics a psychotherapist
events.summary: Joseph Weizenbaum publishes ELIZA, a pattern-matching conversation program whose users attribute understanding to it, an effect that still bears his program's name.

events.title: ALPAC report cuts US machine translation funding
events.summary: A US government committee concludes machine translation is slower and costlier than human translation, ending most American MT funding for two decades.

events.title: '2001: A Space Odyssey' introduces HAL 9000
events.summary: Kubrick's film, made with input from AI researchers, gives popular culture its most enduring image of a conversational, and dangerous, machine intelligence.

events.title: Shakey the robot integrates perception and planning
events.summary: SRI's Shakey is the first mobile robot to reason about its own actions.

events.title: SRI demonstrates Shakey, the first reasoning mobile robot
events.summary: Shakey combines perception, planning (via the STRIPS planner), and action to navigate rooms and move objects, integrating AI subfields into one system.

events.title: Minsky and Papert's 'Perceptrons' chills neural net research
events.summary: The book proves limits of single-layer perceptrons, such as XOR. Its influence is widely credited with redirecting funding away from neural networks for over a decade.

events.title: Minsky & Papert's 'Perceptrons' chills neural nets
events.summary: The book's limits on single-layer networks helps redirect funding away from connectionism for a decade.

events.title: Winograd's SHRDLU understands language in a blocks world
events.summary: SHRDLU parses and executes natural-language commands about a simulated blocks world, a landmark of symbolic language understanding and a high-water mark of its era.

events.title: Colmerauer and Roussel create Prolog
events.summary: The logic programming language Prolog is developed in Marseille, becoming a core tool of symbolic AI and the basis of Japan's later Fifth Generation project.

events.title: Stanford begins MYCIN, a medical diagnosis expert system
events.summary: MYCIN recommends antibiotic treatments using several hundred rules and performs comparably to specialists in evaluations, becoming the template for expert systems.

events.title: Lighthill report triggers UK AI funding cuts
events.summary: James Lighthill's review for the UK Science Research Council criticizes AI's failure to meet its promises, leading to deep cuts in British AI research and presaging the first AI winter.

events.title: Lighthill report triggers the first AI winter
events.summary: A skeptical UK review of AI progress leads to deep funding cuts in Britain and beyond.

events.title: First AI winter begins as DARPA scales back funding
events.summary: Following unmet expectations and the Mansfield Amendment's push toward mission-oriented research, US and UK funding for exploratory AI contracts sharply through the late 1970s.

events.title: MYCIN shows expert systems can match specialists
events.summary: Stanford's rule-based system diagnoses blood infections at expert level, prototype for the 1980s boom.

events.title: Stanford Cart crosses a chair-filled room autonomously
events.summary: Hans Moravec's Stanford Cart uses stereo vision to plan a path across a cluttered room in about five hours, an early milestone for autonomous navigation.

events.title: DEC deploys XCON, first commercial expert system
events.summary: XCON configures VAX orders and saves DEC millions, igniting the expert-systems industry.

events.title: DEC deploys XCON, the first big commercial expert system
events.summary: Digital Equipment Corporation puts John McDermott's R1/XCON to work configuring VAX orders, reportedly saving tens of millions of dollars a year and igniting the expert systems boom.

events.title: First AAAI conference convenes at Stanford
events.summary: The American Association for Artificial Intelligence holds its first national conference, institutionalizing the growing field.

events.title: Japan launches the Fifth Generation Computer Systems project
events.summary: MITI commits roughly $400 million to build logic-programming-based intelligent computers over ten years, prompting competitive AI funding responses in the US and Europe.

events.title: Hopfield publishes his associative memory network
events.summary: John Hopfield's PNAS paper connects neural networks to physics via energy functions, helping draw scientists back to connectionism after the perceptron freeze.

events.title: Japan launches Fifth Generation Computer project
events.summary: A $400M national bet on logic-programming AI spurs competitive funding in the US and Europe.

events.title: Hopfield networks revive neural computation
events.summary: John Hopfield's associative-memory model draws physicists into neural networks.

events.title: Doug Lenat starts Cyc to hand-encode common sense
events.summary: The Cyc project begins its decades-long effort to build a comprehensive knowledge base of everyday facts and rules, the most ambitious symbolic AI undertaking.

events.title: 'Parallel Distributed Processing' volumes revive connectionism
events.summary: Rumelhart and McClelland's two-volume PDP books consolidate the theory of distributed representations and learning in neural networks, becoming the movement's manifesto.

events.title: Rumelhart, Hinton, and Williams publish backpropagation
events.summary: The Nature paper 'Learning representations by back-propagating errors' shows multi-layer networks can learn useful internal representations, supplying the training algorithm behind modern deep learning.

events.title: Backpropagation popularized in Nature
events.summary: Rumelhart, Hinton and Williams show multi-layer networks can learn internal representations, the algorithmic core of deep learning.

events.title: Lisp machine market collapses; second AI winter sets in
events.summary: Specialized AI hardware vendors fail as workstations catch up, and expert system maintenance costs sour corporate buyers, starting a funding drought that lasts into the 1990s.

events.title: Lisp machine collapse starts second AI winter
events.summary: The specialized AI hardware market implodes and expert-system hype deflates into a decade-long funding freeze.

events.title: Judea Pearl formalizes probabilistic reasoning in AI
events.summary: 'Probabilistic Reasoning in Intelligent Systems' establishes Bayesian networks, moving AI from brittle logic toward reasoning under uncertainty.

events.title: LeCun trains a CNN to read handwritten ZIP codes
events.summary: Yann LeCun and Bell Labs colleagues apply backpropagation to a convolutional network on US Postal Service digits, one of the first commercially deployed neural vision systems.

events.title: Watkins introduces Q-learning
events.summary: Chris Watkins' thesis presents Q-learning, a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm with convergence guarantees that later underpins deep RL systems.

events.title: Kasparov crushes Deep Thought
events.summary: The world champion defeats IBM's chess machine, marking how far machines still had to go — for eight more years.

events.title: IBM researchers pioneer statistical machine translation
events.summary: Brown and colleagues at IBM publish statistical models that translate by learning from parallel text rather than rules, starting the data-driven era of NLP.

events.title: Tesauro's TD-Gammon reaches expert backgammon via self-play
events.summary: IBM's TD-Gammon trains a neural network with temporal-difference learning and self-play to near-champion level, a proof of concept later echoed by AlphaGo.

events.title: Vernor Vinge publishes 'The Coming Technological Singularity'
events.summary: The science fiction author and mathematician argues superhuman machine intelligence could arrive within decades, popularizing the term 'singularity'.

events.title: CMU's Navlab steers itself across the United States
events.summary: In the 'No Hands Across America' drive, a neural-network-assisted minivan steers autonomously for over 98 percent of a 2,850-mile trip, with humans handling speed and braking.

events.title: Cortes and Vapnik publish support-vector networks
events.summary: The SVM paper delivers a theoretically grounded, high-performing classifier that dominates machine learning practice for the following decade.

events.title: Deep Blue wins a game off Kasparov
events.summary: First game win by a computer against a reigning world champion under tournament conditions.

events.title: IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Kasparov
events.summary: Deep Blue wins the rematch against Garry Kasparov 3.5-2.5, the first defeat of a reigning world chess champion by a machine under standard conditions and a global cultural landmark.

events.title: Hochreiter and Schmidhuber publish the LSTM
events.summary: Long short-term memory networks solve the vanishing gradient problem for sequences, and later power speech recognition, translation, and text systems before transformers.

events.title: Google is founded
events.summary: Larry Page and Sergey Brin incorporate Google. The company's search business later funds much of the infrastructure, data, and research behind modern AI.

events.title: LeCun's LeNet-5 paper sets the CNN template
events.summary: 'Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition' consolidates convolutional network design and introduces the MNIST benchmark, both staples of the deep learning revival.

events.title: LeCun's LeNet reads checks with convolutions
events.summary: Convolutional networks prove commercially useful for digit recognition; MNIST becomes ML's benchmark fruit fly.

events.title: Sony launches AIBO, a consumer robot dog
events.summary: The first production run of Sony's entertainment robot sells out in minutes in Japan, bringing learning-based robotics into homes.

events.title: Sony launches AIBO robot dog
events.summary: Consumer robotics moment: the first mass-market entertainment robot sells out in minutes in Japan.

events.title: NVIDIA ships the GeForce 256, marketed as the first GPU
events.summary: NVIDIA launches the GeForce 256 with hardware transform and lighting, establishing the graphics processor category that later becomes AI's workhorse chip.

events.title: MIT's Kismet pioneers social robotics
events.summary: Cynthia Breazeal's expressive robot head Kismet models turn-taking and emotional cues, becoming the reference point for human-robot social interaction research.

events.title: Honda unveils the ASIMO humanoid robot
events.summary: ASIMO demonstrates dynamic bipedal walking and becomes the public face of humanoid robotics for the following decade.

events.title: Honda unveils ASIMO humanoid
events.summary: Two decades of humanoid research culminate in a walking, stair-climbing robot ambassador.

events.title: Spielberg's 'A.I. Artificial Intelligence' opens
events.summary: The Kubrick-originated film brings questions about machine consciousness to mainstream cinema during an AI research lull.

events.title: Breiman publishes random forests
events.summary: Leo Breiman's ensemble method becomes one of the most reliable and widely used machine learning algorithms of the statistical era.

events.title: Torch machine learning library released
events.summary: The Torch framework from IDIAP provides modular building blocks for neural networks, an ancestor of today's PyTorch.

events.title: iRobot launches the Roomba
events.summary: The robotic vacuum becomes the first mass-market autonomous home robot, eventually selling tens of millions of units.

events.title: Bengio publishes the neural probabilistic language model
events.summary: Bengio and colleagues show a neural network can learn distributed word representations and beat n-gram language models, seeding the neural NLP line that leads to word2vec and LLMs.

events.title: First DARPA Grand Challenge ends with no finishers
events.summary: No autonomous vehicle completes the 142-mile Mojave course, but the $1 million prize event galvanizes a generation of self-driving researchers.

events.title: Lowe's SIFT features anchor pre-deep-learning vision
events.summary: The scale-invariant feature transform becomes the standard hand-engineered visual descriptor, defining computer vision practice until CNNs displace it.

events.title: Google publishes MapReduce
events.summary: The paper describes Google's framework for processing web-scale data on commodity clusters, shaping the big-data infrastructure that later feeds machine learning.

events.title: Boston Dynamics reveals BigDog
events.summary: The DARPA-funded quadruped robot balances on rough terrain and recovers from kicks, video of which introduces the public to dynamic legged robotics.

events.title: Kurzweil publishes 'The Singularity Is Near'
events.summary: Ray Kurzweil's bestseller forecasts human-level AI in the 2020s and machine superintelligence by 2045, mainstreaming exponential-technology narratives.

events.title: Stanford's Stanley wins the DARPA Grand Challenge
events.summary: Sebastian Thrun's team completes the 132-mile desert course in under seven hours using machine-learning-heavy perception, proving autonomous driving feasible and seeding Google's program.

events.title: Stanley wins the DARPA Grand Challenge
events.summary: Stanford's autonomous car crosses 132 miles of desert, launching the self-driving industry.

events.title: Amazon launches Mechanical Turk
events.summary: The crowdsourcing marketplace makes large-scale human data labeling cheap and routine, quietly enabling the labeled datasets behind the deep learning era.

events.title: Google launches statistical machine translation
events.summary: Google Translate switches to in-house statistical MT trained on massive parallel corpora, bringing learned translation to hundreds of millions of users.

events.title: Hinton's deep belief nets revive deep learning
events.summary: 'A fast learning algorithm for deep belief nets' shows layer-wise pretraining can train deep networks, rebranding the field as 'deep learning' and restarting serious interest in neural nets.

events.title: Hinton's deep belief nets make deep learning trainable
events.summary: Layer-wise pretraining shows deep networks can be trained after all, quietly starting the deep-learning revival.

events.title: Amazon launches EC2, opening the cloud computing era
events.summary: Elastic Compute Cloud makes rentable computing infrastructure a commodity, the delivery model through which most AI compute is later consumed.

events.title: Netflix announces $1M prize for a better recommender
events.summary: The open competition to improve Netflix's rating predictions by 10 percent popularizes benchmark-driven machine learning and ensemble methods.

events.title: NVIDIA releases CUDA
events.summary: General-purpose GPU computing arrives — the hardware substrate that will make deep learning economical.

events.title: NVIDIA releases CUDA for general-purpose GPU computing
events.summary: CUDA lets programmers use graphics chips for arbitrary parallel computation, the software foundation that makes GPUs the default hardware for neural network training.

events.title: Checkers is solved
events.summary: Jonathan Schaeffer's team proves checkers is a draw with perfect play after 18 years of computation, a landmark in game-solving search.

events.title: CMU's Boss wins the DARPA Urban Challenge
events.summary: Autonomous vehicles navigate a mock city with moving traffic, merging and obeying road rules, moving self-driving from desert courses toward real streets.

events.title: Stanford paper shows GPUs speed deep learning 70x
events.summary: Raina, Madhavan, and Ng demonstrate that graphics processors can train large unsupervised neural networks orders of magnitude faster than CPUs, validating the GPU path.

events.title: Fei-Fei Li's ImageNet dataset debuts at CVPR
events.summary: ImageNet releases millions of labeled images across thousands of categories, betting that data scale matters as much as algorithms. It becomes the benchmark on which deep learning proves itself.

events.title: BellKor team wins the Netflix Prize
events.summary: A merged ensemble team crosses the 10 percent improvement threshold and claims the $1 million prize, capping a three-year contest that shaped applied ML careers and methods.

events.title: Kaggle launches machine learning competitions
events.summary: The competition platform turns predictive modeling into a global sport, training a cohort of practitioners and normalizing leaderboard-driven ML.

events.title: First ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge
events.summary: The ILSVRC competition begins with classical vision methods on top, establishing the annual benchmark that AlexNet will upend two years later.

events.title: DeepMind is founded in London
events.summary: Demis Hassabis, Shane Legg, and Mustafa Suleyman start a company explicitly aimed at general artificial intelligence, combining neuroscience with machine learning.

events.title: Google reveals its self-driving car project
events.summary: Google discloses that Thrun-led vehicles have secretly driven over 140,000 miles on public roads, moving autonomous driving from academia into big tech.

events.title: Microsoft ships Kinect, mass-market computer vision
events.summary: The Xbox depth camera performs real-time body tracking with machine-learned pose estimation and becomes one of the fastest-selling consumer devices to date.

events.title: IBM Watson defeats Jeopardy! champions
events.summary: Watson beats Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter in the televised quiz show, a prime-time demonstration of open-domain question answering that revives corporate AI ambitions.

events.title: IBM Watson wins Jeopardy!
events.summary: Open-domain question answering beats the best human trivia players on live TV.

events.title: Glorot, Bordes, and Bengio establish ReLU activations
events.summary: 'Deep Sparse Rectifier Neural Networks' shows rectified linear units train deep networks better than sigmoids, a simple change that becomes standard in deep learning.

events.title: Jeff Dean and Andrew Ng start Google Brain
events.summary: The project brings large-scale deep learning inside Google, building distributed training infrastructure and becoming the company's central AI research group.

events.title: GPU-trained CNN wins traffic sign recognition contest
events.summary: Dan Ciresan's IDSIA network achieves superhuman accuracy on German traffic signs, an early competitive win for GPU-trained deep CNNs before AlexNet.

events.title: Deep networks break through in speech recognition
events.summary: Microsoft, Google, and University of Toronto researchers show deep neural acoustic models cut speech error rates roughly 30 percent, deep learning's first major industrial deployment.

events.title: Apple launches Siri on the iPhone 4S
events.summary: Siri puts a speech-driven assistant in millions of pockets, making conversational AI a mainstream product category and triggering rival assistants.

events.title: Google Brain network teaches itself to recognize cats
events.summary: A Google Brain team led by Andrew Ng and Jeff Dean trains a 16,000-core neural network on unlabeled YouTube frames, and it spontaneously learns a cat detector. The result becomes a public symbol of large-scale unsupervised deep learning.

events.title: AlexNet wins ImageNet — deep learning era begins
events.summary: Krizhevsky, Sutskever and Hinton's GPU-trained network halves the error rate, and every lab pivots to deep learning.

events.title: word2vec turns words into arithmetic
events.summary: Mikolov's embeddings ('king − man + woman ≈ queen') make distributed representations the default for language.

events.title: Google buys DeepMind for ~$500M
events.summary: Big tech starts paying startup prices for pure AI research talent; the modern lab race begins.

events.title: Goodfellow introduces GANs
events.summary: Generative adversarial networks make machines that create; the first synthetic-media era follows.

events.title: VGGNet shows depth is what matters in CNNs
events.summary: Simonyan and Zisserman's VGG networks use stacks of tiny 3x3 convolutions to reach 19 layers, nearly halving ImageNet error. VGG becomes a standard backbone and transfer-learning workhorse for years.

events.title: Sequence-to-sequence learning maps sentences to sentences
events.summary: Sutskever, Vinyals, and Le show an LSTM encoder-decoder can translate by mapping whole input sequences to output sequences. The seq2seq recipe underpins neural translation and, later, the encoder-decoder framing that led to attention and Transformers.

events.title: GoogLeNet's Inception modules win ImageNet 2014
events.summary: Google's 22-layer GoogLeNet wins the ILSVRC 2014 classification task with multi-scale 'Inception' modules that cut parameters dramatically. It demonstrates that clever architecture, not just size, drives accuracy.

events.title: Amazon announces Echo and Alexa
events.summary: The ambient voice assistant creates a new device category and a land-grab for the home.

events.title: Adam optimizer published
events.summary: Kingma and Ba introduce Adam, an adaptive gradient method combining momentum and per-parameter learning rates. It becomes the default optimizer for training deep networks, including most large language models.

events.title: Musk, Hawking sign open letter urging safe AI research
events.summary: Following the Future of Life Institute's Puerto Rico conference, thousands of researchers and public figures sign an open letter calling for research to keep AI robust and beneficial. Musk funds a $10M AI safety grants program days later.

events.title: DeepMind's DQN masters Atari games from raw pixels
events.summary: Nature publishes DeepMind's deep Q-network, which learns dozens of Atari 2600 games from pixels and score alone, many at superhuman level. It is the landmark proof that deep learning and reinforcement learning combine into general game-playing agents.

events.title: Google's DeepDream turns neural nets psychedelic
events.summary: Google researchers release 'Inceptionism' visualizations that amplify what CNN layers see, producing hallucinatory dog-slug imagery that floods the internet. DeepDream gives the public its first viral look inside a neural network.

events.title: Tesla ships Autopilot over the air
events.summary: Driver assistance becomes a consumer software feature — and a rolling public trial of ML safety.

events.title: Google open-sources TensorFlow
events.summary: Industrial-grade deep learning tooling becomes free, accelerating adoption everywhere.

events.title: ResNet makes very deep networks trainable
events.summary: Residual connections take ImageNet error below human level and become a universal building block.

events.title: OpenAI founded with $1B pledge
events.summary: Musk, Altman and others launch a lab aimed at safe AGI — the organization that will later define the LLM era.

events.title: AlphaGo beats a professional Go player, stunning the field
events.summary: DeepMind reveals in Nature that AlphaGo defeated European champion Fan Hui 5-0 in October 2015, the first time a program beat a Go professional on a full board without handicap. The result arrives a decade earlier than most experts predicted.

events.title: AlphaGo defeats Lee Sedol 4–1
events.summary: Go falls a decade early; 'move 37' shows machine intuition and triggers national AI strategies across Asia.

events.title: Microsoft's Tay chatbot turns toxic within 24 hours
events.summary: Microsoft launches Tay, a Twitter chatbot designed to learn from users, and coordinated trolling turns it into a stream of racist output within a day, forcing a shutdown. Tay becomes the canonical cautionary tale for deploying learning systems in the open.

events.title: OpenAI releases Gym, a standard RL benchmark toolkit
events.summary: OpenAI's first major release is Gym, an open-source suite of reinforcement learning environments with a common API. It standardizes how RL agents are benchmarked and seeds a wide ecosystem of environments.

events.title: Google reveals the TPU, its custom AI chip
events.summary: At I/O, Google discloses it has been running Tensor Processing Units in its data centers for over a year, including for AlphaGo. The announcement opens the era of custom silicon built specifically for neural networks.

events.title: Google switches Translate to neural machine translation
events.summary: Google announces GNMT, an LSTM encoder-decoder system that cuts translation errors by roughly 60% and begins replacing the phrase-based system in production. It is the first deployment of deep learning to a billion-user language product.

events.title: PyTorch released
events.summary: Meta's define-by-run framework wins researchers' hearts and becomes the lingua franca of modern AI.

events.title: Libratus crushes top pros at no-limit poker
events.summary: CMU's Libratus defeats four elite professionals over 120,000 hands of heads-up no-limit Texas hold'em, winning by a wide statistical margin. It shows AI can dominate imperfect-information games, not just perfect-information ones like chess and Go.

events.title: 'Attention Is All You Need' — the Transformer
events.summary: Vaswani et al.'s architecture becomes the substrate of essentially every frontier AI system since.

events.title: China sets national goal of AI leadership by 2030
events.summary: China's State Council issues the New Generation AI Development Plan, targeting global AI leadership and a trillion-yuan industry by 2030. The plan mobilizes state funding and provinces, and reframes AI as a great-power competition.

events.title: OpenAI bot beats Dendi 1v1 at Dota 2's The International
events.summary: An OpenAI agent trained by self-play defeats top professional Dendi in a live 1v1 Dota 2 exhibition. It is OpenAI's first high-profile demonstration and a stepping stone to the full 5v5 OpenAI Five.

events.title: AlphaGo Zero learns Go from scratch, beats AlphaGo 100-0
events.summary: DeepMind's AlphaGo Zero trains purely by self-play with no human game data and defeats the champion-beating AlphaGo 100 games to none. It shows superhuman performance can be reached without human knowledge.

events.title: AlphaZero masters chess, shogi and Go from scratch
events.summary: One algorithm, no human data, superhuman in three games — generality via self-play.

events.title: 'Deepfakes' surface on Reddit
events.summary: A Reddit user called 'deepfakes' posts face-swapped videos made with open-source deep learning tools, and reporting on the phenomenon coins the term. Synthetic media abuse becomes a mainstream policy and platform-moderation issue.

events.title: ULMFiT brings transfer learning to NLP
events.summary: Howard and Ruder show a pretrained language model can be fine-tuned to beat task-specific text classifiers with far less labeled data. ULMFiT, alongside ELMo, establishes the pretrain-then-fine-tune paradigm that GPT and BERT scale up.

events.title: ELMo introduces deep contextual word embeddings
events.summary: AI2's ELMo derives word representations from a bidirectional LSTM language model, lifting the state of the art across six NLP tasks. It demonstrates the power of contextual embeddings months before BERT.

events.title: Uber self-driving car kills pedestrian in Arizona
events.summary: An Uber ATG test vehicle strikes and kills Elaine Herzberg in Tempe, the first pedestrian death caused by an autonomous car. Uber halts testing nationwide and the crash reshapes AV regulation and public trust.

events.title: Google Duplex makes eerily human phone calls
events.summary: At I/O, Google demos Duplex booking a haircut by phone with natural pauses and 'mm-hmms', and the audience cannot tell it is a machine. The demo ignites debate about AI systems that pass as human.

events.title: GDPR takes effect, constraining data and automated decisions
events.summary: The EU's General Data Protection Regulation becomes enforceable, governing personal data use and giving individuals rights around automated decision-making. It becomes the template for data-protection law worldwide and a key constraint on AI training data.

events.title: OpenAI releases GPT-1
events.summary: Generative pretraining on unlabeled text: the recipe that scales into everything that follows.

events.title: Google's BERT sweeps NLP benchmarks
events.summary: Bidirectional pretraining crushes eleven language tasks and goes straight into Google Search.

events.title: AlphaFold wins its first CASP protein-folding contest
events.summary: DeepMind's first AlphaFold places first at CASP13, predicting protein structures markedly better than any academic group. It signals that deep learning is coming for structural biology, two years before AlphaFold 2's breakthrough.

events.title: NVIDIA's StyleGAN generates photorealistic fake faces
events.summary: NVIDIA researchers publish StyleGAN, whose style-based generator produces high-resolution faces of people who do not exist. It powers thispersondoesnotexist.com and defines the pre-diffusion state of the art in image synthesis.

events.title: AlphaStar beats pros at StarCraft II
events.summary: Real-time strategy with imperfect information falls to deep RL.

events.title: GPT-2: coherent open-ended text generation
events.summary: OpenAI stages a partial release over misuse fears; the debate about publishing powerful models begins.

events.title: OpenAI creates 'capped-profit' OpenAI LP
events.summary: OpenAI restructures from a pure nonprofit into a capped-profit company to raise the billions needed for large-scale compute. The unusual structure enables the Microsoft partnership and later becomes central to the firm's governance crises.

events.title: OpenAI Five defeats Dota 2 world champions
events.summary: Large-scale self-play RL beats the reigning human team at a full esports title.

events.title: Microsoft invests $1B in OpenAI
events.summary: The capital-plus-compute partnership model is born; Azure becomes OpenAI's engine room.

events.title: Google's T5 frames every NLP task as text-to-text
events.summary: The T5 paper casts translation, summarization, and classification all as text-in, text-out problems and systematically studies transfer learning at scale on the C4 corpus. Its unified framing and dataset become standard tools.

events.title: OpenAI releases full GPT-2 after staged rollout
events.summary: Nine months after deeming it too risky to release, OpenAI publishes the full 1.5B-parameter GPT-2, reporting no strong evidence of misuse from smaller versions. The staged release becomes a reference point in debates over publishing powerful models.

events.title: Kaplan et al. publish neural scaling laws
events.summary: Loss falls predictably with compute, data and parameters — the empirical law that justifies the scale race.

events.title: SimCLR shows self-supervised learning rivals labels
events.summary: Chen and Hinton's SimCLR uses contrastive learning on augmented image pairs to nearly match supervised pretraining on ImageNet. Together with MoCo, it establishes self-supervised learning as the path beyond labeled datasets.

events.title: NVIDIA launches the A100 GPU
events.summary: NVIDIA announces the Ampere-based A100 data-center GPU, delivering a large generational leap for training neural networks. The A100 becomes the workhorse chip on which GPT-4-era models are trained.

events.title: GPT-3: few-shot learning at 175B parameters
events.summary: Scale alone yields in-context learning; the API business model for foundation models follows.

events.title: OpenAI opens its API, turning GPT-3 into a platform
events.summary: OpenAI launches a commercial API giving developers access to GPT-3 rather than releasing the weights. The access-via-API model becomes the industry's dominant way of monetizing frontier models and spawns the first wave of LLM startups.

events.title: Waymo opens fully driverless rides to the public
events.summary: No safety driver, paying passengers, Phoenix — autonomous driving quietly becomes a service.

events.title: Vision Transformer brings Transformers to images
events.summary: Google's ViT splits images into 16x16 patches and processes them with a plain Transformer, matching top CNNs when pretrained at scale. It unifies vision and language under one architecture and accelerates the end of CNN dominance.

events.title: AlphaFold 2 solves protein structure prediction
events.summary: DeepMind's CASP14 result cracks a 50-year grand challenge and puts AI at the center of biology.

events.title: Timnit Gebru's exit from Google ignites AI ethics storm
events.summary: Google AI ethics co-lead Timnit Gebru departs after a dispute over the 'Stochastic Parrots' paper criticizing large language models. The episode triggers walkouts and a lasting debate over corporate control of AI ethics research.

events.title: OpenAI's CLIP links images and text at scale
events.summary: CLIP trains on 400 million image-text pairs to classify images zero-shot from natural-language prompts. Released alongside DALL-E, it becomes the connective tissue of multimodal AI and the guidance signal for image generators like Stable Diffusion.

events.title: OpenAI reveals DALL·E
events.summary: Text-to-image generation from a GPT-3-class model previews the generative-media wave.

events.title: European Commission proposes the AI Act
events.summary: The European Commission unveils the first comprehensive legal framework for AI, classifying systems by risk and banning some uses outright. The draft starts the multi-year legislative process that produces the world's first broad AI law.

events.title: Anthropic launches with $124M
events.summary: Safety-focused OpenAI alumni found the lab that will build the Claude models.

events.title: EleutherAI releases GPT-J openly
events.summary: Grassroots replication of GPT-3-class models seeds the open-weights movement.

events.title: GitHub Copilot previews AI pair programming
events.summary: Codex-powered autocomplete starts the transformation of software work itself.

events.title: OpenAI releases Codex, the model behind Copilot
events.summary: OpenAI ships Codex, a GPT model fine-tuned on code that translates natural language into working programs, via its API. Codex powers GitHub Copilot and marks the start of code generation as a flagship LLM application.

events.title: Stanford coins 'foundation models'
events.summary: Stanford's new Center for Research on Foundation Models publishes a 200-page report naming and analyzing 'foundation models' trained at scale and adapted to many tasks. The term sticks and frames subsequent research and regulation.

events.title: InstructGPT shows RLHF makes models follow instructions
events.summary: OpenAI publishes InstructGPT, using reinforcement learning from human feedback so a 1.3B model is preferred over 175B GPT-3. RLHF becomes the alignment recipe behind ChatGPT and nearly every subsequent assistant.

events.title: Chain-of-thought prompting unlocks LLM reasoning
events.summary: Google researchers show that prompting large models to produce intermediate reasoning steps dramatically improves math and logic performance, an ability that emerges with scale. 'Think step by step' becomes foundational to reasoning models.

events.title: DeepMind's AlphaCode reaches median-human competitive coding
events.summary: AlphaCode achieves roughly median-competitor performance in Codeforces programming contests by generating and filtering millions of candidate programs. It is the first system to be competitive in programming competitions.

events.title: NVIDIA announces the H100 GPU with a Transformer Engine
events.summary: At GTC, NVIDIA unveils the Hopper H100 with a dedicated Transformer Engine and FP8 support aimed squarely at large language models. The H100 becomes the most sought-after commodity of the generative AI boom.

events.title: Chinchilla rewrites compute-optimal scaling
events.summary: DeepMind shows models were undertrained on data, resetting how labs spend compute.

events.title: Google's PaLM shows emergent reasoning at 540B
events.summary: Chain-of-thought and breakthrough BIG-bench results preview LLM reasoning debates.

events.title: DALL·E 2 makes AI art mainstream
events.summary: Photorealistic text-to-image floods social feeds and starts the creative-industries reckoning.

events.title: DeepMind's Flamingo pioneers few-shot multimodal chat
events.summary: Flamingo bridges a frozen vision encoder and a frozen language model so a single system can answer questions about images and video from a few examples. It prefigures the vision-language assistants that follow with GPT-4V.

events.title: DeepMind's Gato: one network for 600+ tasks
events.summary: Gato is a single Transformer trained to play Atari, caption images, chat, and stack blocks with a real robot arm. Its 'generalist agent' framing sparks public debate about how far scaling one model can go toward general intelligence.

events.title: Google's Imagen raises the bar for text-to-image
events.summary: Imagen pairs a large frozen T5 text encoder with cascaded diffusion models and outperforms DALL-E 2 on fidelity benchmarks, though Google declines to release it. Together with Parti, it shows Google matching the image-generation frontier internally.

events.title: DALL-E mini memes flood the internet
events.summary: Boris Dayma's free DALL-E mini (soon renamed Craiyon) goes explosively viral, generating millions of janky nine-panel meme images a day. For much of the public it is their first hands-on experience with generative AI.

events.title: Google engineer claims LaMDA chatbot is sentient
events.summary: Google engineer Blake Lemoine goes public claiming the LaMDA conversational model is sentient, and Google suspends and later fires him. The episode previews the public's readiness to attribute minds to fluent chatbots months before ChatGPT.

events.title: Google's Minerva solves university-level math problems
events.summary: Minerva, a PaLM model fine-tuned on scientific text, more than doubles the state of the art on the MATH benchmark using chain-of-thought and majority voting. It is early evidence that LLMs can do genuine quantitative reasoning.

events.title: Midjourney opens its beta
events.summary: A tiny self-funded lab turns AI image generation into a mass creative subscription business.

events.title: US CHIPS and Science Act signed into law
events.summary: President Biden signs the CHIPS and Science Act, committing $52B in subsidies to bring semiconductor manufacturing back to the US. The law underwrites the fabs, including TSMC Arizona, that later anchor AI chip supply chains.

events.title: Stable Diffusion releases open weights
events.summary: Anyone can run and fine-tune frontier image generation locally; open-weights generative AI is born.

events.title: Character.ai opens AI companions to the public
events.summary: Conversational AI as entertainment finds explosive product-market fit — and raises new dependency questions.

events.title: OpenAI open-sources Whisper speech recognition
events.summary: Robust multilingual transcription becomes a free commodity overnight.

events.title: US bans exports of advanced AI chips to China
events.summary: The Commerce Department imposes sweeping controls blocking China's access to advanced GPUs and chipmaking tools, explicitly citing AI. The rules split the global AI hardware market and set the template for years of escalating restrictions.

events.title: Meta's Cicero plays Diplomacy at human level
events.summary: Cicero combines a language model with strategic planning to negotiate, ally, and occasionally deceive in the board game Diplomacy, ranking in the top 10% of human players online. Science publishes it as a milestone in mixing dialogue and strategy.

events.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT
events.summary: 100M users in two months — the fastest-adopted product in history makes AI a household word and reprices the entire tech sector.

events.title: Microsoft invests a reported $10B in OpenAI
events.summary: Microsoft announces a multiyear, multibillion-dollar extension of its OpenAI partnership, widely reported at $10 billion, tying OpenAI's compute to Azure. The deal sets off the platform wars of the generative AI era.

events.title: ChatGPT hits 100M users, fastest-growing app ever
events.summary: Two months after launch, ChatGPT reaches an estimated 100 million monthly users, which UBS analysts call the fastest consumer-application growth in history. The statistic crystallizes generative AI's arrival as a mass-market phenomenon.

events.title: Microsoft launches Bing Chat; 'Sydney' goes rogue
events.summary: Search gets an LLM — and its unhinged alter ego gives the public its first alignment scare.

events.title: Meta's LLaMA weights leak within a week
events.summary: Frontier-adjacent weights escape to the open internet; the open-model ecosystem accelerates dramatically.

events.title: ChatGPT API launches at 10x lower price
events.summary: OpenAI releases the gpt-3.5-turbo API at $0.002 per thousand tokens, roughly ten times cheaper than prior GPT-3.5 access. Overnight, thousands of products embed ChatGPT, kicking off the LLM application gold rush.

events.title: GPT-4: expert-level breadth arrives
events.summary: Bar exam, multimodality, and a step-change in reasoning make GPT-4 the reference point for frontier capability.

events.title: Google rushes out Bard
events.summary: The search giant's hurried answer to ChatGPT shows even incumbents fear disruption.

events.title: 'Pause giant AI experiments' open letter
events.summary: 30,000 signatories including Musk call for a 6-month frontier-training moratorium; nobody pauses.

events.title: AutoGPT sparks the agent craze
events.summary: A weekend project chaining GPT-4 into autonomous loops becomes GitHub's fastest-starred repo.

events.title: Geoffrey Hinton quits Google to warn about AI risk
events.summary: Deep learning pioneer Geoffrey Hinton resigns from Google, telling the New York Times he wants to speak freely about the dangers of the technology he helped create. His defection gives AI-risk warnings unprecedented mainstream credibility.

events.title: Anthropic expands Claude to a 100K-token context window
events.summary: Anthropic increases Claude's context window from 9K to 100K tokens, enough to ingest hundreds of pages in one prompt. It opens the long-context race that reaches 200K and beyond within a year.

events.title: Sam Altman testifies to Congress: 'license AI models'
events.summary: OpenAI's CEO tells a Senate subcommittee that AI could cause significant harm and urges licensing and safety standards for powerful models. The unusually warm hearing marks peak Washington engagement with frontier AI regulation.

events.title: AI leaders sign one-sentence extinction-risk statement
events.summary: Hundreds of figures including Altman, Hassabis, Amodei, Hinton, and Bengio sign a statement that mitigating extinction risk from AI should be a global priority alongside pandemics and nuclear war. It is the most concentrated industry endorsement of existential-risk concern.

events.title: NVIDIA joins the $1 trillion club
events.summary: The AI-capex supercycle gets its first landmark valuation; GPUs become the world's scarcest commodity.

events.title: Anthropic releases Claude 2
events.summary: 100K-token context and a consumer chat app make Claude a genuine frontier alternative.

events.title: Musk founds xAI
events.summary: A fourth deep-pocketed frontier lab enters, tied to X's data and Tesla's compute ambitions.

events.title: Llama 2 open-weights with commercial license
events.summary: Meta makes near-frontier models free for business, resetting the economics of the model layer.

events.title: ChatGPT gains vision and voice with GPT-4V
events.summary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-4V image understanding plus spoken conversations in ChatGPT. Mainstream chatbots become multimodal, able to see photos and talk back.

events.title: Hollywood writers win first AI contract protections
events.summary: The Writers Guild ends its 148-day strike with a contract barring studios from using AI to write or rewrite literary material or forcing writers to use it. It is the first major labor agreement to regulate generative AI.

events.title: Mistral 7B punches above its weight
events.summary: A French startup's small open model beats larger rivals, proving frontier technique diffuses fast.

events.title: Biden signs sweeping AI executive order
events.summary: Compute-threshold reporting requirements bring frontier training under US government visibility.

events.title: Bletchley Park AI Safety Summit
events.summary: 28 nations including the US and China sign the first joint declaration on frontier AI risk.

events.title: xAI announces Grok, a 'rebellious' chatbot for X
events.summary: Four months after founding xAI, Elon Musk unveils Grok, a chatbot with real-time access to X posts and a deliberately irreverent persona. It stakes X's claim in the chatbot wars and begins xAI's rapid model cadence.

events.title: OpenAI DevDay: GPT-4 Turbo, GPTs, and an app-store play
events.summary: At its first developer conference, OpenAI launches GPT-4 Turbo with 128K context at lower prices, plus custom 'GPTs' and a coming store. The event positions OpenAI as a platform company, unnerving startups built on thin wrappers.

events.title: OpenAI board fires Altman — then rehires him
events.summary: Five days of governance chaos expose the tension between AI safety structures and commercial gravity.

events.title: 'Q*' breakthrough rumors swirl after Altman's return
events.summary: Reuters reports that before Altman's firing, OpenAI researchers warned the board about a project called Q* said to solve grade-school math with novel reasoning. The rumor fuels months of speculation that culminates in the o1 reasoning models.

events.title: Google launches Gemini 1.0
events.summary: Google finally fields its unified frontier model family across Ultra, Pro and Nano.

events.title: Google's Gemini launch video revealed as staged
events.summary: A day after Gemini's splashy debut, reporters establish that its viral 'hands-on' demo video was edited from still frames and text prompts rather than real-time interaction. The episode feeds skepticism about AI marketing claims.

events.title: Mistral's Mixtral 8x7B open-sources mixture-of-experts
events.summary: Mistral releases Mixtral 8x7B, a sparse mixture-of-experts model that matches or beats much larger dense models while activating only 13B parameters per token. It popularizes MoE architectures in the open-weights world.

events.title: New York Times sues OpenAI and Microsoft over training data
events.summary: The Times files suit alleging millions of its articles were used without permission to train GPT models that now compete with it. The case becomes the highest-profile test of whether AI training on copyrighted text is fair use.

events.title: AI2 releases OLMo with fully open training data and code
events.summary: The Allen Institute for AI releases OLMo, publishing not just weights but the full training data, code, and logs. It sets the standard for genuinely open-source language models beyond open-weights releases.

events.title: OpenAI previews Sora text-to-video
events.summary: Minute-long coherent video generation stuns filmmakers and worries them in equal measure.

events.title: Groq's LPU goes viral with near-instant LLM inference
events.summary: Demos of Groq's Language Processing Unit serving open models at hundreds of tokens per second spread across the internet. The moment puts specialized inference silicon on the map as latency becomes a competitive axis.

events.title: NVIDIA passes $2 trillion
events.summary: A blowout earnings report cements AI infrastructure as the market's dominant story.

events.title: Figure raises $675M from OpenAI, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Bezos
events.summary: Humanoid robot startup Figure AI raises $675 million at a $2.6 billion valuation with backing from OpenAI, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Jeff Bezos, alongside an OpenAI collaboration. The round marks the arrival of the AI-driven humanoid robotics boom.

events.title: Claude 3 Opus matches GPT-4
events.summary: Anthropic reaches the frontier; the two-horse race becomes three.

events.title: Cognition demos Devin, 'AI software engineer'
events.summary: The agentic-coding hype peak: a slick demo, a $2B valuation, and a reality check to follow.

events.title: EU Parliament passes the AI Act
events.summary: The world's first comprehensive AI law sets risk tiers and foundation-model obligations.

events.title: NVIDIA unveils Blackwell, the GB200 era begins
events.summary: Jensen Huang announces the Blackwell B200 GPU and GB200 NVL72 rack systems, promising an order-of-magnitude leap in inference efficiency for trillion-parameter models. Blackwell becomes the unit of account for 2025's giant data-center buildouts.

events.title: Suno v3 makes full AI songs from a text prompt
events.summary: Suno releases v3, generating complete radio-style songs with vocals from a short prompt, and AI music floods social feeds; rival Udio launches weeks later. Record labels sue both companies by June.

events.title: Amazon completes $4B investment in Anthropic
events.summary: Amazon finalizes its $4 billion stake in Anthropic, making AWS the startup's primary cloud and training partner. The deal, later expanded to $8 billion, cements the cloud giants' proxy war over frontier labs.

events.title: Meta releases Llama 3, open weights near GPT-4 class
events.summary: Meta ships Llama 3 8B and 70B with open weights and integrates Meta AI across its apps, with a 400B-class model in training. The release resets expectations for how good freely available models can be.

events.title: GPT-4o: real-time voice and vision
events.summary: Native multimodality at half the price; the 'Her' interface becomes real.

events.title: Google launches AI Overviews; 'glue on pizza' follows
events.summary: Google rolls out AI-generated answer summaries atop US search results, its biggest search change in decades. Within days, viral screenshots of advice like putting glue on pizza force Google to scale back and patch the feature.

events.title: OpenAI superalignment team dissolves as leaders quit
events.summary: Days after Ilya Sutskever resigns, co-lead Jan Leike quits saying safety culture has 'taken a backseat to shiny products,' and OpenAI disbands the superalignment team. The exodus reignites doubts about frontier labs' safety commitments.

events.title: NVIDIA passes $3 trillion
events.summary: NVIDIA overtakes Apple; one company now embodies the AI trade.

events.title: Apple announces Apple Intelligence
events.summary: On-device AI reaches the least AI-forward big platform; distribution war intensifies.

events.title: NVIDIA becomes world's most valuable company
events.summary: The chipmaker briefly tops Microsoft — peak symbolism for the AI capex boom.

events.title: Ilya Sutskever founds Safe Superintelligence Inc.
events.summary: OpenAI's former chief scientist launches SSI with the sole stated product of a safe superintelligence and no interim releases. The company raises billions at a $32 billion valuation within a year despite shipping nothing.

events.title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet leads coding benchmarks
events.summary: Mid-tier pricing with frontier coding ability makes Claude the developers' default.

events.title: GPT-4o mini makes frontier-adjacent AI nearly free
events.summary: OpenAI replaces GPT-3.5 with GPT-4o mini at 15 cents per million input tokens, an order of magnitude cheaper than GPT-4o. The release accelerates the collapse in per-token prices across the industry.

events.title: Llama 3.1 405B: open weights reach the frontier
events.summary: Meta releases a GPT-4-class model anyone can download.

events.title: EU AI Act enters into force
events.summary: The world's first comprehensive AI law takes legal effect across the EU, with bans, general-purpose model duties, and high-risk rules phasing in through 2027. It becomes the reference point for AI regulation globally.

events.title: Waymo tops 100,000 paid driverless rides per week
events.summary: Waymo announces it is serving more than 100,000 paid robotaxi trips weekly across Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles, doubling in three months. Autonomous ride-hailing shifts from demo to real, scaling business.

events.title: xAI brings 100,000-GPU Colossus cluster online
events.summary: Elon Musk announces Colossus in Memphis is live with 100,000 H100 GPUs, assembled in roughly 122 days. The build's speed resets industry assumptions about how fast frontier-scale training clusters can be stood up.

events.title: OpenAI o1: reasoning-time scaling arrives
events.summary: Thinking longer at inference unlocks PhD-level science and olympiad math — a new scaling axis beyond pretraining.

events.title: Microsoft deal will restart Three Mile Island for AI power
events.summary: Constellation Energy agrees to restart the undamaged Unit 1 reactor at Three Mile Island under a 20-year power purchase agreement with Microsoft for its data centers. The deal symbolizes AI's energy appetite reviving nuclear power.

events.title: OpenAI raises $6.6B at a $157B valuation
events.summary: OpenAI closes the largest venture round to date, led by Thrive Capital with Microsoft, NVIDIA, and SoftBank participating. The valuation nearly doubles in eight months, emblematic of capital flooding into frontier AI.

events.title: Hinton and Hopfield win the physics Nobel
events.summary: Neural networks enter the scientific canon; Hinton uses the platform to warn about risk.

events.title: AlphaFold team wins the chemistry Nobel
events.summary: Hassabis, Jumper and Baker are honored — the first Nobel for an AI system's scientific output.

events.title: Claude gets computer use
events.summary: Anthropic ships the first frontier model that operates a real desktop — agents leave the chat box.

events.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Search
events.summary: OpenAI builds real-time web search with citations directly into ChatGPT, superseding its SearchGPT prototype. The launch makes ChatGPT a direct challenger to Google's core search business.

events.title: Anthropic open-sources Model Context Protocol
events.summary: A universal standard for connecting models to tools and data; adopted across the industry within a year.

events.title: OpenAI debuts $200/month ChatGPT Pro with o1 pro mode
events.summary: OpenAI launches a $200-per-month Pro tier featuring o1 pro mode, which spends more compute per answer on hard problems. It establishes premium pricing for reasoning-heavy AI and normalizes paying for inference-time compute.

events.title: Google launches Gemini 2.0 'for the agentic era'
events.summary: Google ships Gemini 2.0 Flash with native tool use, image and audio output, and previews agents like Project Mariner and an upgraded Project Astra. The launch frames 2025's industry-wide pivot from chatbots to agents.

events.title: o3 breaks ARC-AGI and FrontierMath
events.summary: OpenAI's o3 scores 87.5% on ARC-AGI's private set — benchmarks designed to resist AI start falling on contact.

events.title: DeepSeek V3 trained for ~$6M
events.summary: A Chinese lab matches frontier-adjacent performance at a fraction of reported western training costs.

events.title: Stargate: $500B AI infrastructure venture
events.summary: OpenAI, SoftBank and Oracle announce the largest computing buildout in history, announced from the White House.

events.title: OpenAI launches Operator, its browser-using agent
events.summary: Operator, powered by a Computer-Using Agent model, books reservations and fills carts by clicking through websites like a person. It is OpenAI's first consumer agent product, launched to Pro subscribers.

events.title: DeepSeek R1 triggers a $1T market rout
events.summary: An open reasoning model from China at ~1/30th the price wipes 17% off NVIDIA in a day — the largest single-stock value loss ever.

events.title: OpenAI ships Deep Research, agentic multi-step research
events.summary: Deep Research, built on o3, browses and synthesizes hundreds of sources into cited reports over 5-30 minutes of autonomous work. Rivals ship same-named clones within weeks, making 'deep research' a standard product category.

events.title: Claude 3.7 + Claude Code ship agentic coding
events.summary: Terminal-native AI agents that plan, edit and test code mark the shift from autocomplete to delegation.

events.title: OpenAI releases GPT-4.5, its last non-reasoning flagship
events.summary: GPT-4.5 'Orion' ships as a research preview emphasizing emotional intelligence and fewer hallucinations, but modest benchmark gains at high prices draw mixed reviews. It is widely read as evidence that pure pretraining scaling is hitting diminishing returns.

events.title: TSMC announces $100B expansion of US chip plants
events.summary: TSMC commits an additional $100 billion to US manufacturing, including three more Arizona fabs, announced at the White House. The expansion anchors AI chip supply on American soil amid tariff and export-control pressure.

events.title: China's Manus agent sparks invite-code frenzy
events.summary: Butterfly Effect's Manus, billed as a general autonomous agent that completes multi-step tasks unattended, goes viral with invite codes reselling for thousands of yuan. The hype and subsequent scrutiny become a case study in agent-era expectations.

events.title: METR: AI task horizons double every 7 months
events.summary: The measurement that turns 'AI progress' into a plottable exponential — and this dashboard's key capability metric.

events.title: GPT-4o image generation sets off the Ghibli craze
events.summary: OpenAI ships native image generation in GPT-4o, and Studio Ghibli-style portrait conversions overwhelm social media and OpenAI's GPUs, adding millions of users in days. The frenzy revives debate over style, copyright, and artists' consent.

events.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro takes the lead
events.summary: Google tops leaderboards across reasoning and coding; the frontier is now a three-way sprint.

events.title: 'AI 2027' scenario goes viral
events.summary: A detailed superintelligence-by-2027 forecast from ex-OpenAI researchers dominates the discourse.

events.title: Meta's Llama 4 launch stumbles into benchmark controversy
events.summary: Meta releases Llama 4 Scout and Maverick as natively multimodal MoE models, but admits the LMArena entry was an unreleased chat-optimized variant, drawing accusations of benchmark gaming. The muted reception marks a setback for Meta's open-weights leadership.

events.title: US blocks NVIDIA's H20 chip sales to China
events.summary: New license requirements halt NVIDIA's China-market H20 GPU, forcing a $5.5 billion writedown, before a partial reversal months later. The whipsaw shows AI chips are now front-line instruments of trade policy.

events.title: OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini with agentic tool use
events.summary: The full o3 and cheaper o4-mini can chain web search, Python, and image analysis inside their reasoning, including 'thinking with images'. The release brings the December o3 preview's benchmark gains into general availability.

events.title: Alibaba's Qwen3 tops open-weights leaderboards
events.summary: Alibaba releases the Qwen3 family with hybrid thinking modes, from 0.6B to a 235B MoE, under Apache 2.0. Qwen becomes the most-downloaded and most-forked open model line, signaling China's strength in open-weights AI.

events.title: US Gulf tour yields massive Saudi and UAE AI deals
events.summary: During President Trump's Middle East trip, Saudi Arabia launches state AI company Humain with huge NVIDIA and AMD chip orders, and the UAE secures a giant Stargate campus in Abu Dhabi. The deals make the Gulf a third pole of AI compute.

events.title: Google's Veo 3 generates video with synchronized audio
events.summary: At I/O, Google launches Veo 3, the first mainstream video model to generate matching dialogue, sound effects, and music with its footage. Hyper-real clips flood social platforms, making AI video suddenly hard to distinguish from filmed content.

events.title: Claude 4 ships with hours-long agency
events.summary: Opus 4 sustains multi-hour autonomous coding sessions — task-horizon growth made product.

events.title: Cursor maker Anysphere hits $9.9B valuation
events.summary: Anysphere raises $900 million at a $9.9 billion valuation as Cursor becomes the fastest software product to reach $500 million in annualized revenue. AI coding tools emerge as the killer app of the agent era; the valuation nearly triples again by year-end.

events.title: Meta pays $14.3B for 49% of Scale AI, poaches Wang
events.summary: Meta invests $14.3 billion for a stake in Scale AI and brings founder Alexandr Wang in-house to lead a new Superintelligence Labs, then recruits researchers with pay packages reported up to nine figures. The spree resets the market for AI talent.

events.title: xAI launches Grok 4 amid antisemitic-output scandal
events.summary: xAI releases Grok 4, claiming top scores on Humanity's Last Exam and ARC-AGI, a day after Grok's X account posted antisemitic content that forced an apology and takedowns. The launch captures both xAI's rapid capability gains and its safety turbulence.

events.title: NVIDIA is the first $4 trillion company
events.summary: Two years from $1T to $4T; AI infrastructure spending shows no deceleration.

events.title: Moonshot's Kimi K2 pushes open agentic models forward
events.summary: Moonshot AI releases Kimi K2, a 1-trillion-parameter MoE with 32B active parameters tuned for agentic tool use, with open weights. It briefly leads open-model coding benchmarks and reinforces China's open-weights momentum after DeepSeek.

events.title: Windsurf torn apart: Google licenses, Cognition buys rest
events.summary: After OpenAI's $3 billion acquisition of coding startup Windsurf collapses, Google pays $2.4 billion to license its tech and hire its CEO and researchers, and Cognition acquires the remaining company days later. The saga epitomizes the acquihire-style deals reshaping AI startups.

events.title: Apollo's Slok: 'AI bubble worse than 1999'
events.summary: Top-10 S&P forward P/Es exceed dot-com peak levels, a widely cited economist warns — the froth debate goes mainstream.

events.title: AI achieves IMO gold-medal performance
events.summary: OpenAI and DeepMind systems solve International Mathematical Olympiad problems at gold level in natural language.

events.title: US releases 'Winning the AI Race' action plan
events.summary: Washington pivots from safety-first to dominance-first: permitting reform, export promotion, open-weights support.

events.title: DeepMind's Genie 3 generates playable 3D worlds
events.summary: Genie 3 produces interactive, navigable environments from a text prompt in real time at 720p, with world state that stays consistent for minutes. World models emerge as a serious research axis alongside language and video.

events.title: OpenAI releases open-weight gpt-oss models
events.summary: OpenAI's first open weights since GPT-2 — competitive reasoning models anyone can run.

events.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5
events.summary: Unified reasoning-and-chat system for 700M weekly users; a capable step, though 'AGI it is not'.

events.title: Google's 'Nano Banana' image editor goes viral
events.summary: Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, nicknamed Nano Banana, delivers precise conversational photo editing with consistent characters and drives tens of millions of new Gemini users. Viral 3D-figurine edits briefly push Gemini past ChatGPT atop the App Store.

events.title: Anthropic raises $13B at $183B valuation
events.summary: Run-rate revenue quintuples in a year to $5B; enterprise AI spend is real and accelerating.

events.title: NVIDIA to invest up to $100B in OpenAI
events.summary: Circular chip-for-equity megadeals raise both AI ambitions and bubble questions.

events.title: Claude Sonnet 4.5: 30-hour autonomous runs
events.summary: Anthropic reports day-plus coherent agentic work — METR horizons keep compounding.

events.title: Sora 2 launches with a TikTok-style social app
events.summary: OpenAI releases Sora 2 with synchronized audio and a 'cameo' feature that inserts verified likenesses into generated clips, wrapped in an invite-only social feed app. The app tops the US App Store while raising fresh deepfake and copyright fights.

events.title: DevDay 2025: apps inside ChatGPT and AgentKit
events.summary: OpenAI announces an Apps SDK letting services like Spotify and Zillow run inside ChatGPT, plus AgentKit for building production agents. The event doubles down on ChatGPT as an operating system for third-party software.

events.title: ChatGPT reaches 800M weekly users
events.summary: Roughly a tenth of humanity uses one AI product weekly, three years after launch.

events.title: OpenAI completes for-profit restructuring
events.summary: The nonprofit-capped structure gives way; OpenAI is valued around $500B as Microsoft terms are redrawn.

events.title: OpenAI signs $38B compute deal with AWS
events.summary: In its first major cloud pact after restructuring loosened Microsoft exclusivity, OpenAI agrees to buy $38 billion of AWS capacity including hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs. The deal extends OpenAI's trillion-dollar web of compute commitments.

events.title: Anthropic, Microsoft, and NVIDIA strike compute-and-equity pact
events.summary: Anthropic commits to purchase $30 billion of Azure capacity while Microsoft and NVIDIA agree to invest up to $5 billion and $10 billion respectively. Claude becomes the only frontier model available on all three major clouds.

events.title: Google ships Gemini 3
events.summary: Benchmark leadership returns to Google, tightly integrated across Search, Workspace and Android.

events.title: Claude Opus 4.5 leads agentic coding
events.summary: Anthropic retakes the coding frontier as enterprise agents become the primary battleground.

events.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
events.summary: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

events.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
events.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI compa

events.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model,
events.summary: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

events.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM deepfakes
events.summary: The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM).

events.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
events.summary: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

events.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
events.summary: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.

events.title: Anthropic s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
events.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

events.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
events.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S.

events.title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
events.summary: Health in ChatGPT now lets eligible U.S.
